title: Finding the relevant decisions for a feature
question: When consulting the log for a new feature, how does the AI find the decisions that actually relate to it?
rationale: Read everything by default, expand along the graph at scale. At today's sizes the AI loads every decision and judges relevance itself — cheap, and catches connections keyword search misses. Above ~150 decisions it seeds by keyword/category then expands along dependsOn/Affects links to pull the whole affected cluster.

options.label: Read all by default, graph-expand at scale

tradeoffs.note: AI judgment catches relations keyword search misses
no index; reuses the existing structure
reading all stops being cheap past ~150 decisions; then seed-and-expand

options.label: Read all, reason over them (no scale path)

tradeoffs.note: wastes context once the log is large
misses related decisions that share no words

options.label: Filter then expand along the graph

tradeoffs.note: pulls dependsOn/Affects neighbors of the seed hits
still bounded by the seed match; a wordless connection never seeds
